chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ يُوصِي بِحَجَّةٍ فَيُحَجُّ عَنْهُ مِنْ غَيْرِ مَوْضِعِهِ أَوْ يُوصِي بِشَيْ‏ءٍ قَلِيلٍ فِي الْحَجِّ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 62 – The one who bequeaths with a Hajj, so he performs Hajj from other than its place, or he bequeaths something little regarding the Hajj

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/219)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/219)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ مَاتَ وَ أَوْصَى بِحَجَّةٍ أَ يَجُوزُ أَنْ يُحَجَّ عَنْهُ مِنْ غَيْرِ الْبَلَدِ الَّذِي مَاتَ فِيهِ فَقَالَ مَا كَانَ دُونَ الْمِيقَاتِ فَلَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Zakariyya Bin Adam who said,

‘I asked Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> about a man who died and bequeathed with a Hajj. Is it allowed that Hajj be performed on his behalf from another city than the one which he died in?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Whatever was besides the juncture, so there is no problem’.
en.sarwar: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr from Zakariya ibn Adam who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who has said in his will to perform al-Hajj on his behalf; if it is permissible to perform al-Hajj for him from a place other than where he has died. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not harmful to perform it from any place before Miqat (place for assuming the state of Ihram).”’
